title: Authentication required error from v1.11
body:
Hi, I'm running skopeo's Docker image, and observed when I run:

`docker run -v $HOME/.docker:$HOME/.docker --rm quay.docker.internal.cba/skopeo/stable:v1.10 inspect docker://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis:7.0.7 --format "{{ .Digest }}"`
I get:
```
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="Loading registries configuration \"/etc/containers/registries.conf\""
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="Loading registries configuration \"/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf\""
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="Trying to access \"123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis:7.0.7\""
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="No credentials matching 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis found in /tmp/auth.json"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="No credentials matching 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis found in /root/.config/containers/auth.json"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="Found credentials for 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /root/.docker/config.json"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg=" No signature storage configuration found for 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis:7.0.7, using built-in default file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="GET https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="Ping https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/ status 401"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:31Z" level=debug msg="GET https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/redis/manifests/7.0.7"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="Content-Type from manifest GET is \"application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json\""
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="GET https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/redis/manifests/sha256:44acefad97bbaf51632748f469c1ebb859f62e2f2bcc96cdf776b8b791ecac1d"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="Content-Type from manifest GET is \"application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json\""
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="Downloading /v2/redis/blobs/sha256:5f2e708d56aa0ee930aad29e8d7ca3dd7d39e89a376082df2361cdaa9f8c492b"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="GET https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/redis/blobs/sha256:5f2e708d56aa0ee930aad29e8d7ca3dd7d39e89a376082df2361cdaa9f8c492b"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="No credentials matching 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis found in /tmp/auth.json"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="No credentials matching 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis found in /root/.config/containers/auth.json"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="Found credentials for 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /root/.docker/config.json"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg=" No signature storage configuration found for 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis:7.0.7, using built-in default file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="GET https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="Ping https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/ status 401"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:32Z" level=debug msg="GET https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/redis/tags/list"
sha256:bb474c35022ca2c5618f4c49ca759bd2c0eea1daf5d934c560bd30092b97b498
```

But since v1.11, I get:

```
root@140a6f4b064d:/# docker run -v $HOME/.docker:$HOME/.docker --rm quay.docker.internal.cba/skopeo/stable:v1.11 inspect docker://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis:7.0.7 --format "{{ .Digest }}" --debug
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="Loading registries configuration \"/etc/containers/registries.conf\""
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="Loading registries configuration \"/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf\""
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="Trying to access \"123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis:7.0.7\""
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="No credentials matching 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis found in /tmp/auth.json"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="No credentials for 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis found"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg=" No signature storage configuration found for 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis:7.0.7, using built-in default file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="GET https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="Ping https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/ status 401"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="GET https://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/v2/redis/manifests/7.0.7"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="Content-Type from manifest GET is \"text/plain; charset=utf-8\""
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=debug msg="Accessing \"123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis:7.0.7\" failed: reading manifest 7.0.7 in 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis: authentication required"
time="2023-02-14T05:16:04Z" level=fatal msg="Error parsing image name \"docker://123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis:7.0.7\": reading manifest 7.0.7 in 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis: authentication required"
```

I am able to run `docker manifest inspect 123456789876.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com/redis:7.0.7` and retrieve the manifest successfully. Any help would be appreciated!

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

I’m afraid this is an unforeseen interaction of an intentional change in Skopeo, and the way the container is set up: if a credential file is specified, _only_ that credential file is used — and the container is built to specify a credential file.

The result is that the container is now _only_ reading `/tmp/auth.json` by default.

So one solution could be to use `-v` to mount the relevant file directly to that path (something like `-v $HOME/.docker/config.json:/tmp/auth.json`).

Another is to explicitly point at the right config file, roughly: `run … skopeo --authfile /root/.docker/config.json …` .
Thanks for the explanation and solution!
